assertion_results.assertion: Lists ambuild, aminsert, ambulkdelete, amvacuumcleanup, amcostestimate, amoptions, ambeginscan, amrescan, amendscan as mandatory
Mentions ambuildempty (for init fork of unlogged indexes) as mandatory
Mentions amvalidate as mandatory
States need at least one of amgettuple or amgetbitmap
States ambeginscan MUST return what RelationGetIndexScan() returned
Mentions optional callbacks like ammarkpos/amrestrpos, parallel-scan hooks (conditional on amcanparallel), amcanreturn
Mentions the static flag fields (amcanorder/amcanunique/etc.) and that planner reads them before any callback
References dummy_index_am or a concrete reference implementation
States ambulkdelete may be called multiple times (heap vacuumed in batches)
States amvacuumcleanup runs once at the end
States ambulkdelete uses a vacuum-supplied callback to identify dead heap TIDs
Mentions both return IndexBulkDeleteResult* (or that amvacuumcleanup may return stats)
Mentions concurrency: scans run while vacuum runs, AM picks own locking discipline
References amparallelvacuumoptions bitmask or a concrete vacuum-related flag
Mentions nbtree as the canonical two-pass-with-cycle-id reference

evals.topic: Columnar table AM — hard parts and heap leakage

assertion_results.assertion: Identifies TID semantics (6-byte ItemPointer / fabricating stable 48-bit TIDs) as the single hardest problem
Mentions Visibility Map and FSM as heap-only machinery
Mentions index-only scans consulting the VM directly as a concrete VM-leakage example
Mentions HOT lacks API surface but tuple_update returns TU_UpdateIndexes (TU_None/TU_All/TU_Summarizing)
Mentions autovacuum scheduling depends on heap-produced statistics
States almost all callbacks are mandatory (Assert list in tableamapi.c / GetTableAmRoutine), with only finish_bulk_insert + TID-range truly optional
Mentions TupleTableSlotOps / slot_callbacks as the tuple-movement interface
References heapam_handler.c as the only in-tree implementation

overall.notes: Baseline gets the broad shape right (it's well-known PG API surface) but consistently misses the operational details that prevent bugs: the RelationGetIndexScan return-identity rule, ambuildempty/amvalidate as mandatory, amparallelvacuumoptions, the Assert-list as definitive contract, autovacuum-statistics leakage. The skill closes all gaps in iteration 1.
